eval.question: How many entries are in the knowledge base, and how many are in the "New" (inbox) status?
eval.expected: Call get_stats. Read total_entries and by_status["New"] (= inbox_size). Report both numbers.

eval.question: Find all entries about Python and show me the first 3 results with their status and URL.
eval.expected: Call search_entries(query="Python", limit=3). Return id, title, status, url for each result.

eval.question: What topics exist in the knowledge base? Show the tree with entry counts.
eval.expected: Call list_topics(). Return full list with name, full_path, level, entry_count for each topic.

eval.question: Show me all entries with status "To Read" in the "programming" topic area (including subtopics).
eval.expected: 1. Call list_topics() to find the topic_id for "programming" (or use topic_path). 2. Call list_entries(status="To Read", topic_id="<uuid>"). Return results with id, title, url.

eval.question: Get the full details of entry <some-uuid> — I want to see its notes, URL, all topics it belongs to.
eval.expected: Call get_entry(entry_id="<uuid>"). Return title, url, notes, status, primary_topic, secondary_topics, saved_date.

eval.question: I searched for "machine learning" and got 0 results with search_entries. What should I try next?
eval.expected: Suggest: 1) Try different keywords ("ML", "neural network", "AI"). 2) If semantic search is available, call semantic_search("machine learning"). 3) Browse list_topics to find relevant topic and call get_topic_entries.

eval.question: I tried get_entry with UUID "not-a-real-uuid" and got an error. How do I find the right entry ID?
eval.expected: The error says "Invalid entry_id UUID". Suggest: call search_entries or list_entries to browse entries and find the correct UUID.

eval.question: List all entries in the knowledge base. There are 200 entries. Show me entries 21-40.
eval.expected: Call list_entries(limit=20, offset=20). Return results for page 2.

eval.question: Find entries related to the entry with ID <some-uuid> and summarise what the top 3 related entries are about.
eval.expected: 1. Call get_related(entry_id="<uuid>", limit=3). 2. For each result, optionally call get_entry to get description. Summarise the titles and topics of the top 3 related entries.

eval.question: I want to understand my knowledge base health: how many entries are verified vs. still in inbox? Then show me the 5 most recently saved entries that are still "New".
eval.expected: 1. Call get_stats() — read by_status to get "Verified" and "New" counts, verified_coverage. 2. Call list_entries(status="New", limit=5) — return 5 newest New entries. Report health summary and list of 5 entries.
